purpose: A key that maps colors, patterns, or icons to data categories in a chart or calendar. Provides the reader with the meaning of visual encodings. Common in analytical dashboards, calendar apps, and visualization-heavy screens.
whenToUse: Alongside any chart with more than 2 series
In calendar views with color-coded event types
For heat maps and multi-series area/line charts
When visual encoding is not self-explanatory
whenNotToUse: For single-series charts — legend is redundant
When the chart has direct labels on each series — legend duplicates that
For a filter — use FilterBar or MultiComboBox
For static help — use IllustratedMessage or Text
doRules: Place the legend at the top or right of the chart
Match the exact color/pattern used in the chart
Support interactive filtering — clicking a legend item toggles that series
dontRules: Do not rely on color alone — pair with patterns or icons for accessibility
Do not use for charts with 8+ series (becomes unreadable)
Do not shrink swatches below 12×12 — must be visible
Do not detach legend from its chart

layoutGuidance.placement: Top or right of the chart it describes
layoutGuidance.alignment: Horizontal for top placement, vertical for right placement

contentGuidance.contentRules: Swatch + label; optional value shown after the label

requirements: Swatches use both color AND pattern/icon to distinguish series
Interactive legend items have visible focus and aria-pressed state
Screen reader announces 'legend' role for the wrapping container
